solutions.solution: d; None of them is implicit. The statement only implies that if a boy is very honest, it would not be possible for him to lie. But I is just the opposite of it. Assumption II is not implicit because the statement does not talk about 'dishonest' boys.

b; X air-conditioner has the largest range. But, this does not mean that it is the only brand to have a wide range. So, I is not implicit. But II is. If air-conditioners are being advertised, a demand for them must be existing. Advertisements are meant for gaining investment. So I is implicit. But II is not.
a; 'Sole agent' is the highlight of the advertisement. So I is implicit. But II is not.

d; None is implicit. The statement only says that opening a library in Rambli would be a wastage. But it does not say why! I and II are only guesses. Maybe, opening a library in Rambli is a wastage because there is already a library there. (It is an example of a long-drawn conclusion.)
